questions.explanation: A DBMS manages data storage, retrieval, concurrency, and access control so multiple users/applications can safely use the same data.

options.A: Render web pages and handle HTTP requests
options.B: Store, organize, and provide controlled access to data
options.C: Compile application source code into binaries
options.D: Encrypt all files on a server by default

questions.question: What is the primary role of a Database Management System (DBMS)?

questions.explanation: A primary key uniquely identifies each row and cannot contain NULL values.

options.A: Rows must be unique and not NULL for the key column(s)
options.B: Values must match a pattern defined by a regular expression
options.C: The table must be stored in sorted order on disk
options.D: All columns in the table must be encrypted

questions.question: In a relational database, what does a PRIMARY KEY enforce?

questions.explanation: MySQL uses the SQL statement CREATE DATABASE to create a new database schema container.
questions.question: In MySQL, which statement is commonly used to create a new database?

questions.explanation: top provides an interactive, real-time view of processes and system resource usage.
questions.question: On Linux, which command is typically used to view real-time CPU and memory usage interactively?

questions.explanation: A junction (bridge) table with two foreign keys correctly represents many-to-many relationships and supports constraints and indexing.

options.A: Add a Courses column to Students containing a comma-separated list of course IDs
options.B: Add a Students column to Courses containing a comma-separated list of student IDs
options.C: Create a junction table (e.g., Enrollments) with foreign keys to Students and Courses
options.D: Duplicate student rows for each course in the Students table

questions.question: You need to model a many-to-many relationship between Students and Courses. What is the standard relational design approach?

questions.explanation: A composite index starting with the filter column (last_name) can efficiently satisfy both the WHERE predicate and the ORDER BY on first_name.

options.C: An index on (last_name) only, because ORDER BY never uses indexes

questions.question: A query frequently filters on last_name and then sorts by first_name: WHERE last_name = ? ORDER BY first_name. Which index is generally most appropriate?

questions.explanation: Log Shipping automates transaction log backup, copy, and restore to secondary servers to maintain a warm standby for disaster recovery.
questions.question: In SQL Server, which feature is primarily used to automatically capture and ship transaction log backups to one or more secondary servers for warm standby?

questions.explanation: PITR in PostgreSQL requires a base backup plus archived WAL segments covering the recovery window so you can replay changes to a target timestamp.

options.A: Take a logical dump (pg_dump) once per day and disable WAL archiving
options.B: Enable WAL archiving and take a base backup, then retain archived WAL segments for at least 24 hours
options.C: Rely only on streaming replication without any backups
options.D: Use TRUNCATE on large tables nightly to reduce recovery time

questions.question: A PostgreSQL database must support point-in-time recovery (PITR) to any moment in the last 24 hours. Which backup strategy best meets this requirement?

questions.explanation: If the working set exceeds memory, increasing buffer/cache can reduce physical reads and I/O waits, improving latency without application changes.

options.A: Increase the database buffer/cache memory allocation (and ensure OS has enough RAM)
options.B: Disable all indexes to reduce write overhead
options.C: Switch all tables to a text-based storage format
options.D: Force the optimizer to use nested loop joins for all queries

questions.question: You are investigating a sudden increase in latency. Most time is spent waiting on disk I/O, and the working set no longer fits in memory. Which change is most likely to reduce I/O waits without changing application code?

questions.explanation: Asynchronous replication allows replicas to lag behind the primary while scaling read workloads and reducing commit latency on the primary.

options.A: Synchronous replication to all replicas with zero lag
options.C: Two-phase commit across all replicas for every transaction
options.D: Manual export/import using CSV files only

questions.question: A company needs to replicate data from a primary database to multiple read-only replicas, and replicas may lag slightly behind the primary. Which replication mode best fits this requirement?
